Cosmoteer path not set, please set it in: Cosmoteer path not set, please set it in the extensions settings for Cosmoteer Rules Configuration. If you dont see this setting, than please restart vscode. This is required for the language server to work correctly.
Add \"{0}\" to the mod's strings files: Add "{0}" to the mod's strings files
Could not find Cosmoteer installation au: Could not find Cosmoteer installation automatically, please set the path manually
Invalid cosmoteer path, the path should: Invalid cosmoteer path, the path should end with common or Cosmoteer or Data
Could not read the Cosmoteer installatio: Could not read the Cosmoteer installation at {0}, please set the path manually
Each action patches the effective game t: Each action patches the effective game tree. ✓ the target exists, ✗ it resolves to nothing (the action does nothing in game), · existence is not required (create/ignore flag).
⚠ {0} action(s) have a target that resol: ⚠ {0} action(s) have a target that resolves to nothing — they silently do nothing in game.
{0} of {1} `.rules` files are reachable: {0} of {1} `.rules` files are reachable from the manifest (action sources, their includes and inheritance, and the strings folder). The game never loads the rest.
Dead content: backups and templates are: Dead content: backups and templates are expected here, but a part or effect you meant to ship should not be.
ℹ The root `cosmoteer.rules` here is a d: ℹ The root `cosmoteer.rules` here is a documentation convention: the game injects such globals via the manifest actions into its own `Data/cosmoteer.rules` and never loads the mod's copy.
{0} of these are referenced by nothing a: {0} of these are referenced by nothing at all. {1} are referenced only from other unreachable files or from commented-out lines (shown as ←), so wiring in the root of such a chain revives every file behind it.

not found in any strings file: not found in any strings file
The game reads this whole line as one te: The game reads this whole line as one text element
In a list, a value runs to the end of th: In a list, a value runs to the end of the line and '{0}' does not end it, so '{1}' and the body become a single text element. List elements cannot have names; remove '{1}' to make this an anonymous element.
The game reads this as ONE element, \"{0: The game reads this as ONE element, "{0}". Separate the elements with "," or ";", or put each on its own line
References in function calls need to be: References in function calls need to be parenthesized or math expressions
The asset \"{0}\" could not be found, re: The asset "{0}" could not be found, relative to this file or any inherited asset base
References can be in the following forma: References can be in the following formats: <>, .., ~, /, ^, &<>, &.., &~, &/, &A-Z
The relative path does not resolve from: The relative path does not resolve from this file. "{0}" resolves from the game folder and works from any file.
You either reference a non-existing iden: You either reference a non-existing identifier or an identifier that is not in scope
Fragile relative path into the workshop: Fragile relative path into the workshop folder
This path resolves relative to this file: This path resolves relative to this file and breaks when the file moves. "{0}" resolves from the game folder and works from any file.
Unknown shader constant '{0}'. The shade: Unknown shader constant '{0}'. The shader '{1}' declares no such uniform.
The line break already ends this entry,: The line break already ends this entry, so the separator has no effect. Separators are only needed between entries on the same line
'{0}' has no value. The game reads a val: '{0}' has no value. The game reads a valueless field as null and fails to load it into a non-nullable {1}.
'{0}' has the wrong casing. The game's e: '{0}' has the wrong casing. The game's enum parsing is case-sensitive; write '{1}'.
'{0}' is not a valid {1}. Expected one o: '{0}' is not a valid {1}. Expected one of: {2}
'{0}' was renamed to '{1}' in a newer ga: '{0}' was renamed to '{1}' in a newer game version ({2}).
Expected a whole number, but this value: Expected a whole number, but this value is {0}.
{0} reads only the first {1} list elemen: {0} reads only the first {1} list elements, so the game never reads this one.
'{0}' is not a member of {1}, so the gam: '{0}' is not a member of {1}, so the game never reads it here. It is a field of the enclosing group and belongs outside the '{2}' brackets.
'{0}' is not a member of {1}, so the game never reads it here.
'{0}' is a {1} field; the game does not: '{0}' is a {1} field; the game does not evaluate math here and reads the value as literal text.
'{0}' is a {1} field. The game cannot re: '{0}' is a {1} field. The game cannot read a {2} value here.
'{0}' reads only {1} list elements, so t: '{0}' reads only {1} list elements, so the game never reads this one.

Mod action cannot target a language stri: Mod action cannot target a language string file
Files under the \"StringsFolder\" (such: Files under the "StringsFolder" (such as "en.rules") are not modifiable by actions; provide your own per-language string file instead
Add action is missing the Name field: Add action is missing the Name field
Adding to a whole \".rules\" file or a g: Adding to a whole ".rules" file or a group "{ }" requires a "Name" for the new entry
Mod action cannot target a whole .rules: Mod action cannot target a whole .rules file
The \"{0}\" action must target a node in: The "{0}" action must target a node inside a ".rules" file, not the file itself
Mod action target has the wrong shape: Mod action target has the wrong shape
Two operators in a row in a math express: Two operators in a row in a math expression
There should be a value (number or refer: There should be a value (number or reference) between two operators
A math expression cannot end with an ope: A math expression cannot end with an operator
Add a value (number or reference) after: Add a value (number or reference) after the trailing operator
Invalid argument type, expected Number o: Invalid argument type, expected Number or Reference. Got {0}
Math expressions can only contain number: Math expressions can only contain numbers and references ("&"), not a {0}
No localization key \"{0}\" is defined i: No localization key "{0}" is defined in any strings file.
This inheritance chain eventually refers: This inheritance chain eventually refers back to itself, so it can never be resolved
'{0}' is not a member of {1} and is neve: '{0}' is not a member of {1} and is never referenced in this file, so the game ignores it.
The \"{0}\" function takes exactly {1} a: The "{0}" function takes exactly {1} argument(s), but got {2}
The \"{0}\" function takes at least {1}: The "{0}" function takes at least {1} argument(s), but got {2}
\"{0}\" is not a known math function: "{0}" is not a known math function
Reference in function calls needs to sta: Reference in function calls needs to start with an ampersand
Write the argument as \"&{0}\" so it is: Write the argument as "&{0}" so it is read as a reference
Reference in function calls needs to be: Reference in function calls needs to be parenthesized
Wrap the reference in parentheses, e.g. "({0})", to separate it from the other arguments
Invalid argument type, expected Referenc: Invalid argument type, expected Reference(&) or Number. Got {0}
Function-call arguments must be a number: Function-call arguments must be a number or a reference ("&"), not a {0}
\"{0}\" is defined more than once in thi: "{0}" is defined more than once in this scope; only the last definition takes effect
Remove the quotes — a \"&\" reference is: Remove the quotes — a "&" reference is written without quotation marks
Reference should start with an ampersand: Reference should start with an ampersand
The game reads everything up to the line: The game reads everything up to the line end as a single value. Separate the fields with "," or ";", or move the second field to its own line
Expected right brace but found end of fi: Expected right brace but found end of file
Expected right brace to close the group: Expected right brace to close the group
Not expected right brace, did you mean t: Not expected right brace, did you mean to open a group?
Expected right bracket but found end of: Expected right bracket but found end of file
Expected right bracket to close the list: Expected right bracket to close the list
Not expected bracket, did you mean to op: Not expected bracket, did you mean to open a list?
Expected value, expression or function c: Expected value, expression or function call
Values can be a number or a reference, e: Values can be a number or a reference, expressions can be +, -, *, /
Expected value or expression in math exp: Expected value or expression in math expression
If you want to assign a value to an iden: If you want to assign a value to an identifier, you need to provide a value after the equals sign
If you don't want to assign a value to a: If you don't want to assign a value to an identifier, you need to remove the equals sign
The game cannot read a standalone refere: The game cannot read a standalone reference here
A bare reference is only allowed as a li: A bare reference is only allowed as a list element or as a field value (Field = &/Path). The game fails to load the whole file on this.
Those Values should be a References: Those Values should be a References

String delimiters are only allowed after: String delimiters are only allowed after a String
